architecture_analysis_sufficiency.graphify_verdict: corpus is large enough that graph structure adds value (per GRAPH_REPORT.md)
concerns: Only 5 Python source files (~321 LOC) — very small for rich architecture narrative
No formal package layout (flat scripts in mathsquiz/ and polygons/)
No unit tests in target repo — failing-test proximity feature will need wrapper tests
Code appears Python 2-style in places (invalid Python 3 syntax) — debugging-focused, not production architecture
Graphify AST-only run produced 100% EXTRACTED edges; no INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS edges without LLM semantic pass on README docs
architecture_analysis_sufficiency.too_small_risk: Moderate. Graphify still produced 26 nodes and communities, but god-node/bottleneck story may be thin. Token-efficiency demo may show little savings on such a tiny corpus. Assignment is still feasible if we add project-level tests and rich Obsidian narrative.
architecture_analysis_sufficiency.recommendation: Continue with broken-python unless lecturer requires larger codebase; do not switch automatically.
